questions.question: Piston pin is also known as:

explanations.a: Crank pin connects the connecting rod to the crankshaft.
explanations.b: The piston pin is commonly known as the gudgeon pin.
explanations.c: Fastening pin is a general term and not specific.
explanations.d: Connecting pin is not a standard engine term.

questions.question: The small end of the connecting rod attaches to:

answers.c: The piston pin, gudgeon pin or wrist pin

explanations.a: Crank pin is connected to the big end.
explanations.c: The small end is connected to the piston through the piston (gudgeon/wrist) pin.
explanations.d: Connecting pin is not used here.

questions.question: The big end of the connecting rod connects to the:

answers.d: Bearing journal on the crank throw

explanations.a: Wrist pin is connected to the small end.
explanations.b: Rod bolts hold the cap only.
explanations.d: The big end connects to the crankshaft at the bearing journal.

questions.question: Connecting rods for internal combustion engines need to be:

explanations.b: Medium weight is not a design requirement.
explanations.c: Connecting rods must be light to reduce inertia and improve efficiency.

questions.question: Popular materials used in the construction of connecting rods are:

explanations.a: Copper and brass are not suitable for connecting rods.
explanations.b: Plastic and rubber cannot withstand engine stresses.
explanations.c: Wood and fiber are unsuitable materials.
explanations.d: Titanium and aluminium are commonly used due to their high strength-to-weight ratio.
